aliases: Chicken Dumplings with Chestnut and Cranberry

english: Chicken Dumplings with Chestnut and Cranberry

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic dumpling class; no specific Christmas chicken dumpling entry exists. Closest defensible match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKP covers dumplings generically; no festive or fusion-specific code exists. Closest defensible code used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for chicken dumplings as a food product; left null to maintain ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of Christmas Chicken Dumplings is approximately 150 g, equivalent to about 6 medium dumplings, as commonly plated in a restaurant or festive setting.

nutrient_source.title: Christmas Chicken Dumplings — Singapore HPB Foodo Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Christmas Chicken Dumplings (cr_id G00010302). All core macros and micronutrients are taken from this canonical HPB record. Saturated fat and dietary fibre are not reported in the HPB source; saturated fat is estimated from USDA FoodData Central entry for chicken dumpling (USDA FDC ID 1100185, 'Dumpling, chicken') and dietary fibre is estimated from USDA FDC ID 1100185 as the closest approved-source analogue. Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, trans fat, and several micronutrients are not available in either source and are set to -1.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al., American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Christmas Chicken Dumplings. GI estimated at 48 (low) based on the closest analogous food: wheat-flour-wrapped steamed dumplings (jiaozi/dim sum dumplings) from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2021 international GI tables, which report GI values of 46–50 for steamed wheat-flour dumplings with meat filling. Glycemic load per 150 g serving calculated as: GL = (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (48 × 12.05) / 100 ≈ 5.8.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium at 46 mg/100 g with supportive potassium at 82 mg/100 g
hypertension.guidance: At 46 mg sodium per 100 g, Christmas Chicken Dumplings are well within the low-sodium threshold; however, patients with hypertension should avoid high-sodium dipping sauces such as soy sauce or chili sauce, which can substantially increase total sodium intake per meal.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low carbohydrate (8.03 g/100 g), low GI (~48), negligible sugar (0.21 g/100 g), and high protein (8.71 g/100 g) moderating postprandial glucose response
type2_diabetes.guidance: The low glycemic index (~48) and low carbohydrate content of 8 g per 100 g, combined with 8.7 g protein per 100 g, make this dish suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes when consumed in moderate portions; patients should avoid sweet dipping sauces and monitor total carbohydrate load from the wheat flour wrappers.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low total fat (3.78 g/100 g), estimated low saturated fat (~1.0 g/100 g), and low sodium (46 mg/100 g)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The low total fat and estimated saturated fat content make Christmas Chicken Dumplings a heart-friendly option; individuals with cardiovascular disease should confirm that fried chicken skin is excluded or minimised, as skin significantly raises saturated fat, and should use low-sodium condiments to preserve the dish's favourable sodium profile.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Christmas Chicken Dumplings are a festive fusion dish served in Singapore, combining the traditional Chinese dumpling (jiaozi) format with Western Christmas-season ingredients. Each dumpling is made with a thin wheat flour wrapper encasing a filling of minced chicken, roasted chestnut, cranberry, and cheddar cheese, seasoned with herbs. The dish is typically accompanied by celeriac puree, a cider jus, pickled pomegranates, and fried chicken skin as garnish, creating a complex interplay of savoury, tangy, and slightly sweet flavours with a tender, juicy interior and a soft, slightly chewy wrapper. Nutritionally, the dish is relatively lean at 101 kcal per 100 g, with 8.71 g protein, 3.78 g fat, and 8.03 g carbohydrate. Sodium is very low at 46 mg per 100 g, making it suitable for sodium-restricted diets when consumed without high-sodium condiments. The estimated glycemic index of approximately 48 places it in the low-GI category, supporting blood glucose management for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes. The dish is not suitable for vegetarians, vegans, or those with gluten or dairy allergies. It is commonly served as a main course at lunch or dinner during the Christmas festive season in Singapore restaurants.
common_user_queries: How many calories in Christmas Chicken Dumplings?
Are Christmas Chicken Dumplings suitable for diabetics?
What is the sodium content of Christmas Chicken Dumplings?
What is the glycemic index of chicken dumplings?
Can I eat Christmas Chicken Dumplings if I have hypertension?

confidence_reason: Core macros sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo canonical database entry (cr_id G00010302, confidence 0.95). Saturated fat and dietary fibre estimated from USDA FoodData Central closest analogue (chicken dumpling) and documented. GI estimated from University of Sydney GI Database analogous food (steamed wheat-flour meat dumplings) with clear documentation. All sources are approved under Project Veritas v2 source policy.
notes: 1. Saturated fat (1.0 g/100 g) is estimated from USDA FoodData Central FDC ID 1100185 (Dumpling, chicken) as the closest approved-source analogue; the HPB source does not report saturated fat for this entry. 2. Dietary fibre (1.5 g/100 g) is estimated from USDA FDC ID 1100185 as the HPB source does not report fibre for this entry. 3. Monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, vitamin panel, and moisture data for this specific festive fusion dumpling preparation are not available in the HPB Singapore Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026. 4. No core macro is zero; all four core macros (energy, protein, fat, carbohydrate) are non-zero and sourced from HPB. 5. GI of 48 is an analogous estimate based on steamed wheat-flour meat dumplings from the University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson et al. 2021; no direct GI measurement for this specific dish exists. 6. Halal status is not confirmed; cider jus may contain alcohol and cheddar may use animal rennet — consumers following halal dietary requirements should verify with the vendor. 7. The input record listed ethnic_tags including 'Japanese' which is not supported by the dish description or ingredients; this tag has been removed as the dish is a Chinese-fusion preparation served in Singapore.
